issue.title: Perf: cut desktop cold-start — code-split the ~1.5MB bundle (lazy-load non-terminal views + manualChunks)

successCriteria.description: Production build emits multiple chunks; the initial/main chunk is materially smaller than the current single ~1.5MB chunk (record the delta)
tsc -b (pnpm build) clean and pnpm -C apps/desktop test clean
All views (terminal, kanban, dependencies, settings) and the detail panel still render correctly; lazy ones load on first navigation/selection
App launches and navigates without broken/blank views in tauri:dev
successCriteria.note: code-complete; awaiting visual confirmation in tauri:dev

phases.title: vite manualChunks vendor split
phases.content: Record the BASELINE bundle: run `pnpm -C apps/desktop build` and note the main chunk size (currently ~1.5MB, single chunk, with the >500kB warning). Then add `build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks` to apps/desktop/vite.config.ts splitting node_modules vendors into logical groups so they become separate cacheable chunks: e.g. `react`/`react-dom` -> 'react'; `@xyflow/react` + `dagre` -> 'graph'; `@xterm/*` -> 'xterm'; `react-markdown` + `rehype-highlight` + `highlight.js` + `remark-gfm` -> 'markdown'; `framer-motion` -> 'motion'; `@dnd-kit/*` -> 'dnd'. Use a function form that checks `id.includes('node_modules/<pkg>')`. Rebuild and confirm multiple vendor chunks are emitted and the main chunk shrank. NOTE: manualChunks alone is a modest win for a local-loaded Tauri app — the larger cold-start win comes from phases 2-3 deferring EVAL via lazy. This phase is independent of App.tsx (different file).
verification: vite.config.ts has build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks splitting the heavy vendors
pnpm -C apps/desktop build emits multiple vendor chunks (graph/xterm/markdown/etc.)
main chunk size recorded before vs after
phases.summary: Added build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks to vite.config.ts (graph/xterm/markdown/motion/dnd/react groups). Single 1576.96kB chunk -> split: index 287.80kB + vendor chunks; >500kB warning gone. Deferral of graph/dnd/markdown off cold-start comes in P2/P3.

phases.title: React.lazy + first-visit gate for non-default views (Kanban, Dependencies, Settings)
phases.content: In apps/desktop/src/App.tsx, convert the KanbanBoard, DependencyGraph, and SettingsPage imports to `React.lazy(() => import(...))`. CRITICAL: these views are currently always-mounted and toggled via a `hidden` CSS class (App.tsx:367-385), so React.lazy ALONE would still load every chunk at mount (they're all in the JSX tree). Add a first-visit gate: track which views have been activated (e.g. `const [visited, setVisited] = useState<Set<ViewType>>(() => new Set(['terminal']))`; in an effect on `activeView`, add the current view to the set). Render each lazy view's <section> body ONLY if `visited.has(view)` (so its chunk loads on first navigation), wrapped in <Suspense fallback={<small loader>}>. Once visited, keep it mounted (the `hidden` class still toggles visibility) so view state persists. TerminalPane stays EAGER and always-mounted (it is the default view AND must keep PTYs alive — do NOT gate or lazy it). Keep the section wrappers/`hidden` classes intact so layout/visibility behavior is unchanged. Edits App.tsx (shared with phase 3).
verification: KanbanBoard, DependencyGraph, SettingsPage are React.lazy + Suspense-wrapped
each non-terminal view renders null until first visited, then stays mounted
tsc -b clean; switching to each view renders it (no blank/broken view)
phases.summary: App.tsx: KanbanBoard/DependencyGraph/SettingsPage converted to React.lazy; added visitedViews first-visit gate (terminal pre-seeded) so each non-terminal view chunk loads only on first navigation, then stays mounted (hidden toggle). TerminalPane stays eager. Sections wrapped in Suspense. Verified by P3 final build.

phases.title: React.lazy the detail panel + final gate + bundle delta
phases.content: In apps/desktop/src/App.tsx, convert the detail-panel component imports (IssueDetail, PullRequestDetail, ReleaseDetail, TikiReleaseDetail, ResearchDetail — the `detail/` barrel pulls react-markdown + rehype-highlight + highlight.js via MarkdownRenderer) to `React.lazy`, wrapped in <Suspense>. The detail panel only renders when something is selected, so this defers the markdown/highlight bundle until a detail is first opened. (DetailEmptyState is tiny — can stay eager.) Then the FINAL gate: `pnpm build` (full workspace tsc -b) clean, `pnpm -C apps/desktop test` clean, and re-measure the production bundle — confirm the INITIAL chunk shrank materially vs the phase-1 baseline (record the delta in the summary). Visual SC4 (app launches + every view/detail renders in tauri:dev) is user-verified. Edits App.tsx (shared with phase 2 — serialized via dependency).
verification: detail components are React.lazy + Suspense-wrapped; markdown chunk deferred until a detail opens
initial bundle chunk materially smaller than baseline (delta recorded)
(visual, user) app launches and all views/detail render in tauri:dev
phases.summary: App.tsx detail panels (IssueDetail/PullRequestDetail/ReleaseDetail/TikiReleaseDetail/ResearchDetail) React.lazy + Suspense; DetailEmptyState stays eager. BUNDLE DELTA: single 1576.96kB chunk -> entry 191.58kB; graph(267)+dnd(48)+markdown(334)=649kB now lazy (load on first nav/detail). Cold-start JS ~828kB vs 1577kB (~47% less). pnpm build clean; desktop vitest 208 passed.
